title: helianthemum polifolium var. calcareum
intro: Helianthemum polifolium var. calcareum, commonly known as rockrose, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Cistaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as soothing skin irritations and reducing fever, due to its antiseptic and antipyretic properties. Rockrose can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or salve, and has also been used in traditional medicine to treat wounds and skin conditions, although its efficacy and safety as a treatment have not been extensively scientifically studied.
